adopting_body.description: The corporate body that ADOPTED the certificate of resumption. Mutually-exclusive enum: 'members' | 'directors'. 13-B nonprofit-specific (vs CORP_MBCA-14A which only has shareholders); whether members or directors is the legally-correct adopting body depends on the entity's bylaws and whether the corporation has voting members.

adoption_date.description: Date the FIRST adoption action occurred (whether at a meeting or by written consent). Form-scoped naming consistent with resumption.adopting_body / resumption.method. Distinct from CORP_MBCA-14A.resumption.meeting_date which is only meaningful for the meeting branch — on the nonprofit template the date label sits BEFORE the meeting/consent split.

adoption_location.description: Location of the FIRST adoption action. Required when resumption.method='meeting'; conventionally the place of signing when resumption.method='written_consent' (often blank). Distinct from CORP_MBCA-14A.resumption.meeting_location for the same reason as adoption_date.

voting_body.description: The corporate body that VOTED to resume activities (SECOND certification). Mutually-exclusive enum: 'members' | 'directors'. Should match resumption.adopting_body in normal cases — distinct widget retained because the form has separate checkboxes that could in theory be filled inconsistently; rubric cross-checks the two.

line1.description: First (inline) line of the corporation's Maine registered-office address as stated on the form. Two-widget free-text per the MARK1 .line{1,2} convention; the label '(street, city, state and zip code)' is shared. Distinct from registered_agent.physical_address — registered_office.address is the corporation's own registered office, NOT the registered agent's address.

line2.description: Second (continuation) line of the corporation's Maine registered-office address. Companion to registered_office.address.line1.

entities.description: Entity name(s) the filing covers, as listed on the cover block.
